_comment: ISOLATION EXPERIMENT C (FULL) — companion to isolation_trim.json. IDENTICAL Constant_Sigma setup (Re=1e5, alpha_0=0.1, Da=40, uniform σ_c) EXCEPT experimental_reaction_mode='remove_tau' which, via the harness toggle at run_test.jl, selects ProjectFullResidual: the reaction σu is LEFT IN the stabilization (exactly the Forchheimer/ProjectFullResidual behaviour). NB 'remove_tau' is only a not-'standard' sentinel here — it has NO effect on the adjoint or τ in this harness's solve path (get_reaction_mode_trait is consulted only in diagnostics_helpers.jl, not the residual/Jacobian). So TRIM vs FULL differ ONLY in whether the reaction is in the stabilization. If FULL folds while TRIM converges ⇒ reaction-in-stabilization is the cause of the low-α fold (σ̃_α coercivity collapse); Taylor-Hood, having no stabilization term, never pays this.
